Analysis: fixing per-task LR reset should improve continual acquisition. To reduce task imbalance, add optional balanced joint fine-tuning on all seen/all tasks after sequential training, with validation checkpointing. Decision: update train.py incrementally with LR reset plus joint_epochs support, train 2 epochs/task plus 8 balanced joint epochs, then submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: the 8-joint-epoch ViT run improved hidden accuracy from 0.9308 to 0.9336 and best validation accuracy from 0.9342 to 0.9384, indicating balanced joint fine-tuning is still helping, though gains are diminishing. Analysis: the current ViT pipeline is stable, validation-checkpointed, and the prior run cost ~20 minutes, leaving enough time for a longer run. A 16-joint-epoch run is a low-risk extension that may improve representation/calibration while best-val checkpointing guards against late overfit. Decision: train the same ViT configuration with 16 joint epochs into a new checkpoint directory and submit its best checkpoint immediately.

Decision: update predict.py to automatically ensemble the two best ViT checkpoints when available while preserving single-checkpoint fallback, then submit checkpoints_vit_j8/best.pt.
